issues.title: Duplicate InlineEditor directory in studio app
issues.description: Two nearly-identical directories exist side by side: `InlineEditor/` (PascalCase) and `inline-editor/` (kebab-case), each containing 47 files. Production code imports from `InlineEditor/` only. The `inline-editor/` folder appears to be an unresolved rename/migration artifact. Both folders are tracked in git, resulting in ~47 duplicate source files that must be kept in sync manually.
issues.fix: Remove the unused `inline-editor/` (kebab-case) directory. Confirm no imports reference it, then delete the folder and its test files.

issues.title: 'use client' placed after eslint-disable comments
issues.description: In several files, one or more `/* eslint-disable */` block comments appear before the `'use client'` directive. While Next.js accepts comments before `'use client'`, the project standard (and `@next/next/first-file-directive` lint rule intent) is for `'use client'` to appear on line 1 as the very first statement for clarity and tooling consistency.
issues.fix: Move `'use client'` to the very first line of each file, then place the eslint-disable comments after it.

issues.title: Exported components missing named Props interface (inline props)
issues.description: Several exported components use inline object type destructuring instead of a named `ComponentNameProps` interface. This prevents the props type from being imported by consumers, reduces IDE discoverability, and violates the component patterns convention.
issues.fix: Extract the inline object type into a named interface (e.g. `interface LangTextareaProps { ... }`) co-located with the component, and reference it in the function signature.

issues.title: `as any` used for react-hook-form dynamic nested field paths
issues.description: Multiple InlineEditor components (SectionCard, SectionItemsAccordion, SectionItemsCards, SectionItemsGallery, SectionItemsTwoColumn, InlineEditor) use `as any` to pass dynamically constructed field path strings to `useController`/`useFieldArray`. The casts have `/* eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any */` guards with explanatory comments, but the underlying issue is that `react-hook-form` cannot infer types for template-literal paths. This is a widespread pattern inside the InlineEditor sub-feature.
issues.rule: No `any` type usage in production components
issues.fix: Consider creating a typed helper such as `type SectionItemPath<T> = ...` using template-literal types from the form schema, or using `useWatch` with a typed selector. If the cast cannot be avoided, keep the existing eslint-disable with comment. The approach is already documented inline, so this is informational.

issues.title: `type TranslatorFn = (...args: any[]) => string` in CartItemRow
issues.description: A `TranslatorFn` type using `any[]` parameters is defined to type the `next-intl` translator passed as a prop. This pattern works but loses type safety. The `useTranslations` return type from `next-intl` is a publicly exported `TranslationValues`-based callable that can be typed more precisely.
issues.fix: Import `useTranslations` return type from `next-intl` (available as `ReturnType<typeof useTranslations>`), or use `Parameters<typeof t>` to derive the signature, eliminating the `any[]`.

issues.title: Internal render helper function defined inside component body
issues.description: In `SectionCard.tsx` and `DynamicFormField.tsx`, a `renderItems()` / `renderInput()` function is defined inside the component body. Per the standard component structure template, the render phase should be a single JSX block; conditional rendering switch logic should either be extracted to a sibling function outside the component or converted to a dedicated sub-component.
issues.fix: Move `renderItems()` / `renderInput()` outside the component function as a plain function that accepts the required props, or extract into a dedicated presentational component.

issues.title: Private compound component helper props typed inline without interface
issues.description: Several private internal compound component helpers (e.g. `AccordionItemEditor`, `GalleryItemEditor`, `FileInput`) use inline object types for their props instead of named interfaces. While these helpers are not exported (acceptable exception per one-component-per-file rule), giving them named interfaces improves readability and IDE support.
issues.fix: Add a private named interface (e.g. `interface AccordionItemEditorProps { ... }`) above each internal helper function to document the expected props shape.

patterns.description: All production components using React hooks (useState, useEffect, useCallback) correctly include the `'use client'` directive. No missing directives were found across the entire apps/ directory.
Component structure consistently follows hooks → derived state → handlers → render order across reviewed files (UsersPageContent, AssignedOrdersPageContent, OrderCard, CartItemRow, SectionCard).

Named Props interfaces (ComponentNameProps pattern) are consistently used for exported public components across admin, store, payments, and auth apps. The pattern is missing only in a small subset of studio/InlineEditor components (COMP-003).

Container/Presenter pattern is well applied. All PageContent components perform data fetching via hooks and pass results as props to pure UI components (e.g. UsersPageContent → UserTable, AssignedOrdersPageContent → ReceivedOrderCard).

`as any` usage in InlineEditor components is consistently guarded by `eslint-disable` comments with detailed justifications referencing the react-hook-form template-literal path limitation. The casts are intentional and documented.

The `inline-editor` (kebab-case) folder in studio is a full duplicate of `InlineEditor` (PascalCase). Both appear in git history from the same initial commit. Production imports use only `InlineEditor`. The kebab-case folder may be an artifact of a file-rename operation on a case-insensitive filesystem.
